text: The CT scan depicted an extensive involvement of the left lower lobe by the process.
It has been provoking several episodes of pneumonia over the years to the patient.
We routinely imply the triportal Copenhagen approach
A thoracoscopy stiff adhesions were found between the involved lobe, the diaphragm and the mediastinum.
A high energy device proved very useful during the dissection, providing proper hemostasis on the adhesions.
After having freed the lung parenchyma, we approached the fissure in order to dissect the branches of the pulmonary artery to the lower lobe.
The vessels were surrounded by enlarged lymph nodes.
A markedly enlarged bronchial artery may be seen in the posterior aspect of the pulmonary hilum, just behind the apical segmental artery.
After the section had been completed, the vessel was sutured with a stapling device.
A pathological bronchial artery was also found within the pulmonary ligament.
It was isolated and closed with a high energy device, allowing safe isolation and ligation of the lower pulmonary vein.
The next steps consisted in completion of the fissure and closure of the bronchus.
The specimen was extracted with an endo bag.
At the end of the procedure a single chest tube was inserted through the posterior axis.
